name: Google DESIGN.md
application_guidance: Use this to anchor DESIGN.md structure, token contracts, and lint-friendly accessibility sections; pair it with visual references when shaping the final aesthetic direction.

name: VoltAgent awesome-design-md
application_guidance: Use this to compare homepage structure, narrative flow, and hierarchy patterns across many design docs; translate the best recurring moves into original project-specific sections and layouts.

name: awesome-design-skills
application_guidance: Use this for agent workflow design, quality gates, and repeatable design-document procedures; combine it with visual systems when defining the final interface look.

name: awesome-claude-design
application_guidance: Use this for fast AI design-package flow, preview scaffolding, and Claude-compatible handoff patterns; strengthen final docs with stricter token, accessibility, and source-specific guidance.

name: design-md-for-ai
application_guidance: Use this as a secondary comparison corpus for style patterns and DESIGN.md organization; triangulate with stronger sources before choosing the project’s main direction.

name: awesome-ios-design-md
application_guidance: Use this for mobile-first hierarchy, native-feeling interaction, and motion expectations; adapt platform principles to the product’s actual responsive and desktop needs.

name: Material Design 3
application_guidance: Use this as a dependable baseline for tokens, component behavior, elevation, and accessibility; add project-specific personality through brand voice, spacing rhythm, and visual emphasis.

name: Shopify Polaris
application_guidance: Use this for trustworthy commerce/admin flows, forms, validation, and decision clarity; adapt the confidence and usability patterns into marketing pages with a lighter brand-specific presentation.

name: IBM Carbon Design System
application_guidance: Use this for enterprise credibility, dense information hierarchy, data visualization, and accessibility discipline; scale the weight and visual density to match the product’s audience and conversion goal.

name: Microsoft Fluent 2
application_guidance: Use this for familiar productivity patterns, cross-platform component behavior, and enterprise polish; pair it with a distinct brand direction so the homepage feels specific rather than generic.

name: Ant Design
application_guidance: Use this for admin, dashboard, table, and form-heavy product surfaces; translate the density into only the sections that need operational depth while keeping landing pages focused.

name: shadcn/ui ecosystem
application_guidance: Use this for modern React/Tailwind component defaults and developer-friendly product surfaces; make the result distinctive with project-specific hierarchy, tokens, copy, and composition.

name: GitHub Primer
application_guidance: Use this for developer-product clarity, dense navigation, documentation structure, and workflow proof; translate repository patterns into project-specific information architecture, tokens, and original interface composition.

name: Radix UI Primitives
application_guidance: Use this as a behavioral reference for accessible primitives, keyboard flow, state, and focus management; pair it with a visual/token source to shape the final aesthetic system.

name: GOV.UK Design System
application_guidance: Use this for plain-language service clarity, form confidence, and accessibility-first journeys; translate its service patterns into the product’s own identity, content model, and page composition.

name: U.S. Web Design System
application_guidance: Use this for mobile-accessible service structure, regulated-product clarity, and content hierarchy; soften the institutional feel with brand-specific tokens, voice, and visual pacing.

name: Adobe Spectrum / React Spectrum
application_guidance: Use this for accessible complex-app behavior, adaptive components, and creative-product workflows; express those patterns through original product identity, iconography, and visual language.

name: Chakra UI
application_guidance: Use this for pragmatic accessible React surfaces, theme token structure, and fast SaaS assembly; create distinction through project-specific hierarchy, rhythm, and brand tokens.

name: Mantine
application_guidance: Use this for product UI breadth, form/settings patterns, and dark-mode token planning; pair it with brand and landing references to give the homepage a distinct personality.

name: Open Props
strengths: broad CSS custom property token set
application_guidance: Use this for CSS variable architecture, spacing scales, typography primitives, and motion defaults; combine it with component and accessibility sources to form a complete design system.

name: Headless UI
application_guidance: Use this for accessible interaction behavior in custom-branded Tailwind components; pair it with visual references so DESIGN.md defines both behavior and aesthetic direction.

name: Elastic UI Framework
application_guidance: Use this for data-heavy dashboard hierarchy, filtering, search, and observability workflows; adapt the interaction model into original tokens, components, and information design.

name: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
license: W3C Document License / W3C Software License
application_guidance: Use this to ground dialogs, menus, tabs, accordions, and other widgets in correct semantics, keyboard behavior, and focus flow; layer visual design from separate brand sources.

name: WCAG Quick Reference
application_guidance: Use this to convert accessibility criteria into concrete product rules for contrast, content, forms, motion, and keyboard access; keep the generated DESIGN.md practical and design-oriented.

name: Inclusive Components
application_guidance: Use this for inclusive component reasoning, edge cases, and human-centered tradeoffs; translate the insights into original patterns, copy guidance, and QA checks.

name: The A11Y Project
license: open-source project / site content terms vary

live_sources.license: open-source project / site content terms vary

application_guidance: Use this for team-readable accessibility onboarding and broad QA framing; validate normative requirements against WCAG and WAI-ARIA while keeping guidance practical for builders.

name: Design Tokens Community Group Specification
application_guidance: Use this for token naming, types, and cross-tool interoperability; connect the architecture to project-specific color, type, spacing, and component decisions.

name: Style Dictionary
application_guidance: Use this for multi-platform token export and implementation handoff; pair it with visual and accessibility sources so the docs describe both design intent and build path.

name: Tailwind CSS
application_guidance: Use this for implementation-ready theme mapping, responsive spacing, and utility composition; create a distinctive identity through custom tokens, components, and content hierarchy.

name: Panda CSS
application_guidance: Use this for typed token systems, component recipes, and scalable theme architecture; connect the implementation model to clear visual intent and user-facing design rules.

name: Park UI
application_guidance: Use this for modern accessible component aesthetics and recipe-based design-system docs; adapt component patterns through project-specific tokens and original demo compositions.

name: Salesforce Lightning Design System
application_guidance: Use this for enterprise workflow clarity, CRM-style data entry, record pages, and mature component taxonomy; translate patterns into original assets, styling, and product-specific flows.

name: Atlassian Design System
application_guidance: Use this for collaboration-product structure, productivity workflows, and well-documented token ideas; adapt concepts into original naming, styling, illustrations, and page composition.

name: Apple Human Interface Guidelines
application_guidance: Use this for platform-native interaction principles, mobile constraints, input behavior, and motion expectations; express them through the product’s own identity, layout, and marketing voice.

name: Motion for JavaScript / React
application_guidance: Use this to define purposeful interaction motion, layout transitions, gestures, and presence patterns; include reduced-motion, performance, and restraint rules in the final design docs.

name: Razorpay Blade Design System
strengths: cross-platform React and React Native coverage
application_guidance: Use this for fintech-grade component discipline, payment workflow clarity, and cross-platform token structure; translate its operational patterns into original merchant, finance, or trust-heavy product interfaces.

name: Semi Design
application_guidance: Use this for global enterprise surfaces, token-rich component systems, and internationalized admin UX; adapt its i18n, dark-mode, and DesignOps patterns into project-specific product architecture.

name: Vant Mobile UI
application_guidance: Use this for mobile commerce density, app-like interaction controls, and checkout-adjacent form behavior; convert its component taxonomy into original mobile-first product flows.

name: Singapore Government Design System
application_guidance: Use this for accessible public-service structure, official content hierarchy, and trust-heavy forms; translate the service patterns into product-specific IA while keeping government identity cues out of private brands.

name: Base UI
application_guidance: Use this as a behavior layer for original visual systems: extract focus, state, and ARIA interaction models, then pair them with project-specific tokens and visual references.

name: React Aria
application_guidance: Use this for inclusive interaction behavior and internationalized component semantics; combine its accessibility models with project-owned styling, copy, and layout decisions.

name: Web Awesome
application_guidance: Use this for framework-neutral component strategy and themeable web UI; focus on open-core component taxonomy and CSS variable patterns while keeping premium assets and identity choices separate.

name: Utopia Fluid Responsive Design
application_guidance: Use this to create fluid typography and spacing rules that scale across devices; translate its formulas into project token ranges and verify readability, zoom, and layout stability.

name: W3C WAI Designing for Web Accessibility
strengths: bridge from WCAG to design decisions
application_guidance: Use this as a designer-readable accessibility checklist; turn its tips into concrete color, label, feedback, navigation, and motion requirements inside project-specific design docs.

name: Observable Plot
application_guidance: Use this for clear exploratory chart structure and dashboard storytelling; map its marks, scales, and transforms into product-specific chart guidance and tokenized visual hierarchy.

name: Vega-Lite
application_guidance: Use this when Artic needs AI-readable chart contracts and repeatable dashboard grammar; connect declarative encodings to project tokens, accessibility labels, and interaction rules.

name: Tokens Studio for Figma
application_guidance: Use this to bridge design and code token workflows; translate Figma sync, semantic token, and theme management patterns into Artic handoff docs without making Figma tooling mandatory.

name: Growth.Design Landing Page UX Psychology
license: Public web content; principle extraction only
application_guidance: Use this to turn landing-page psychology into practical section rules: reduce competing CTAs, group related proof near claims, and make the first screen earn attention before asking for conversion.

name: Marketing Examples Landing Page Guide
license: Public web content; principle extraction only
application_guidance: Use this to make Artic hero sections sharper: state the outcome in the headline, explain the mechanism in the subtitle, show the product doing useful work, and phrase CTAs around user value.

name: Google PAIR People + AI Guidebook
license: Public Google guidebook; principle extraction only
application_guidance: Use this for AI product pages that need more than magic-language: explain what the system does well, where users stay in control, how feedback improves outcomes, and how limitations are handled gracefully.

name: Microsoft HAX Human-AI Interaction Guidelines
license: Public Microsoft research guidance; principle extraction only
application_guidance: Use this to keep AI homepage claims credible: pair autonomy promises with capability boundaries, correction paths, confidence cues, and user-control language that supports calibrated trust.

name: Diátaxis Documentation Framework
application_guidance: Use this for developer-tool homepages where docs are part of conversion: separate learning, task completion, reference lookup, and conceptual explanation instead of collapsing every CTA into a generic docs link.

name: Figma Pricing Page Best Practices
license: Public Figma resource; principle extraction only
application_guidance: Use this to shape SaaS pricing sections around clear plan count, visible tradeoffs, transparent billing language, recommendation cues, and FAQ coverage for common buyer objections.

name: Cloudscape Design System
application_guidance: Use this when a homepage must make the product feel operationally trustworthy; translate cloud-console structure, resource management, and status patterns into original product previews and dashboard sections.

name: LINE Design System
license: Public design-system docs; brand assets governed by LINE terms
strengths: components and case studies for service ecosystems
application_guidance: Use this for approachable Asia-market mobile experiences; extract interaction grammar, tone, and service-hub patterns while replacing LINE-specific color, icon, and brand identity with project-specific tokens.

name: KRDS Korea Design System
license: Korean public design-system docs; verify asset-specific terms
strengths: forms, login, search, and application flows
application_guidance: Use this to add Korean trust, accessibility, and service-flow discipline to fintech, onboarding, and application-heavy pages; transform public-service clarity into project-specific product language.

name: TDesign Tencent Enterprise Design System
application_guidance: Use this for products that must stay coherent across desktop admin, mobile, and mini-program surfaces; extract taxonomy and theming patterns rather than Tencent-specific visual identity.

name: PatternFly
application_guidance: Use this to make complex product surfaces legible: reduce PatternFly's enterprise density for homepage previews, but keep its navigation, toolbar, table, and dashboard grammar as credibility signals.

name: Ark UI
application_guidance: Use this to give Artic component specs a reliable behavior and accessibility layer; combine Ark's headless contracts with a separate visual source for tokens, tone, and layout.
